content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 13, 2002

The Honorable Wesley Chesbro
Senator – 2nd District
State Capitol
Post Office Box 942848
Sacramento, CA 94248-0001

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-152

Dear Senator Chesbro:

This letter is in response to the request for written advice from George J. Bennett of the
Covelo Community Services District, which was forwarded by your office. The request pertains
to the doctrine of incompatible offices, which is not under our purview. The Fair Political
Practices Commission advises on laws only pertaining to the Political Reform Act.1 Moreover, it
appears the letter may have been forwarded to us in error. It is addressed to the Office of the
Attorney General. We have enclosed a copy of California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B),
which explains our procedures for Mr. Arge or his authorized representative to seek assistance
from the Fair Political Practices Commission.

Should Mr. Arge have a question under the Act, we encourage him to write the
Commission directly for an analysis.

If you have any questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

Enclosure
LM:jg
cc: George J. Bennett

1. Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission does not have purview over the doctrine of incompatible offices, which is not governed by the Political Reform Act. The request appears to have been forwarded to the FPPC in error and should be directed to the Office of the Attorney General instead.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an individual can hold multiple offices simultaneously under the doctrine of incompatible offices.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the question concerns the doctrine of incompatible offices, which falls outside the FPPC's jurisdiction as it is not covered by the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Request was forwarded in error; letter should have gone to Attorney General. Document references enclosure of California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether an individual can hold multiple offices simultaneously under the doctrine of incompatible offices.

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission does not have purview over the doctrine of incompatible offices, which is not governed by the Political Reform Act. The request appears to have been forwarded to the FPPC in error and should be directed to the Office of the Attorney General instead.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines a request for advice forwarded by Senator Chesbro on behalf of George J. Bennett regarding incompatible offices, as this doctrine is outside the Commission's purview under the Political Reform Act.
